title: List for Web back home — sophomore-spring logistics dump (textbooks, financial aid, CS 4-year plan)
html:
TL;DR

  • Sophomore-spring (March 31, 2022) packing-and-admin list captured at OSU — pillows, fan, underwear, air filter, plus a Top Hat textbook line — written as Yahir prepped to go back home to Grants Pass for the break.
  • Action items split between financial-aid cleanup (cancel out-of-business-school tuition, deal with incidental fee) and switching the major to CS (build the four-year plan, decide whether to take ENGR 102 in spring or summer).
  • One of the earliest in-vault artifacts confirming the business-to-CS pivot — Yahir is actively unwinding business-college tuition charges, not just thinking about the switch.
  • Single-line "send out all of the emails regarding finances and books etc" — the standard Yahir admin-batch pattern (collect dread, batch-execute).
  • Title "Web" is shorthand for Grants Pass / home (likely a phone autocorrect on "we" or family-shorthand) — paired with "back home" it's the going-home checklist.

Key fragments

"Go to financial aid office cancel out business tuition / incidental fee"

"Make the 4 year plan for CS / determine if I should take engr 102 now / Or wait until the summer"

Why it matters

This is one of the timestamps for the business → CS major switch at OSU. It's not a manifesto note — it's the chore list around the switch, which is more reliable evidence than a journal entry. The fact that he's canceling business-college tuition in late March 2022 means the decision was already made by then, even if the formal program change came later. Pair with the OSIG workbook (same window) to triangulate the moment Yahir's college identity reshapes from finance-kid to CS-kid while keeping the value investing instinct intact via OSIG.

Open questions

  • What was "Top Hat" — the classroom-response textbook platform, or a class nickname?
  • Did the ENGR 102 decision get made before or after the "Instead of OSIG" redirect a couple weeks later?
